channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US-China Cooperation Raises Global Concerns Over Hidden Costs
item.description: The recent summit between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping marks a new phase in the relationship between the world's two largest economies. While the term 'G2' once symbolise...

item.title: Vincent Co reportedly in the Philippines amid wedding rumors with Bea Alonzo in Spain
item.description: Reports surfaced on May 15, 2026, that Vincent Co was seen in the Philippines on the same day that rumors suggested he was getting married to actress Bea Alonzo in Spain. Despite circulating speculati...

item.title: Iran sets conditions for renewed US talks and asserts control over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tensions between Iran and the United States remain high as Tehran has outlined five conditions for returning to negotiations, including reparations for the ongoing conflict and formal recognition of I...

item.title: Ombudsman Suspends Senate Sergeant-at-Arms Mao Aplasca for 6 Months After Firing Incident
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman, led by Jesus Crispin Remulla, has placed acting Senate Sergeant-at-Arms Mao Aplasca under a preventive suspension of six months following a shooting incident inside the Se...

item.title: Court Approves Withdrawal of Corruption Charges Against Former Migori Governor Okoth Obado After Asset Forfeiture Deal
item.description: Former Migori Governor Zachary Okoth Obado has achieved a significant legal success after a Nairobi anti-corruption court approved the withdrawal of all corruption charges against him and 17 other co-...

item.title: Fitch affirms Globus Bank’s strong financial health with stable outlook
item.description: Fitch don give Globus Bank Long-Term Issuer Default Rating (IDR) of ‘B-’ with Stable Outlook, plus Viability Rating of ‘b-’ and National Long-Term Rating of ‘BBB(nga)’ with Stable Outlook. Dem show sa...

item.title: CRDB Bank and Tanzania Mining Commission Launch Financing for Small-Scale Gold Miners
item.description: The Tanzania Mining Commission has officially signed a financing agreement with CRDB Bank to enhance access to capital for small-scale gold miners in Tanzania. The program targets improved productivit...

item.title: Puscifer and A Perfect Circle announce European and UK tours for 2026
item.description: Puscifer have confirmed their 2026 UK and European tour, marking their first performances in the region for three years. The tour follows the release of their new, heavier album 'Normal Isn’t' and the...

item.title: Former Lawmaker Warns Supreme Court Ruling Could Undermine Minority Voting Rights
item.description: G.K. Butterfield Jr., a former Democratic representative from North Carolina, has raised concerns about a recent Supreme Court decision in Louisiana v. Callais, describing it as a 'five-alarm fire' fo...

item.title: Security Forces Repel Terrorist Attack on Bajaur Army Camp, Four Soldiers Martyred
item.description: On Thursday night, an attempted attack by terrorists on an army camp in Bajaur, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, was successfully repulsed by security forces. According to official sources, nine terrorists were ki...

item.title: Exploring Women’s Sexuality: From Antiquity to Modern Times
item.description: The article examines the evolving perceptions of women’s sexuality from antiquity to the present day. In ancient Greece and Rome, women were often viewed as more sexual than men, a belief reflected in...

item.title: David Keter wins Emurua Dikirr by-election with strong mandate
item.description: David Keter of the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) has clinched the Emurua Dikirr parliamentary by-election after securing 18,266 votes, defeating DCP's Vincent Rotich who garnered 10,760 votes. The ...

item.title: Baryo HiFi Festival Celebrates Filipino Music, Cuisine, and Culture in Historic Filipinotown
item.description: Baryo HiFi, a festival that highlights Filipino heritage, has officially returned to Historic Filipinotown, bringing together music, food, and culture for the local and wider community. The event aims...

item.title: Trump and Xi Hold Summit in China; Xi Gains Strategic Advantage
item.description: During this week’s summit in China, U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping met to discuss trade, Iran, and Taiwan, achieving minor progress while avoiding major conflict. Trump high...

item.title: FG Transfers Foreign Nationals Suspected of Terror Links From Illegal Mining to NSA
item.description: The Minister of Solid Minerals Development, Dele Alake, don talk say dem don hand over foreign nationals wey dem catch for illegal mining and wey dem dey suspect say e fit get connection with terroris...

item.title: US Judge Temporarily Blocks Sanctions on UN Expert on Palestine
item.description: In a notable legal development, a US federal judge has temporarily halted sanctions imposed last year on Francesca Albanese, the UN Human Rights Council Special Rapporteur on the occupied Palestinian ...

item.title: Onopa Community Celebrates King Victor Diemeze Awala as New Atissa Monarch
item.description: HRH Tamama Morris (Ogbo IX), paramount ruler of Onopa Community in Yenagoa LGA, Bayelsa State, don commend HRM King Victor Diemeze Awala (Ebeni-Ebe VIII) for smooth takeover of Atissa Kingdom throne. ...

item.title: Strengthening African CSOs: Practical Guide for Sustainability and Resilience
item.description: This article reviews 'Building on Solid Ground: Primer on Resilience and Sustainability of CSOs in Africa' by Udo Jude Ilo. The book dey guide civil society organisations (CSOs) for Africa on how dem ...

item.title: Tanzania explores economic potential of 14 million tonnes of annual waste
item.description: Tanzania produces over 14 million tonnes of waste every year, with big towns like Dar es Salaam, Mwanza, Arusha, Dodoma, Mbeya and Tanga contributing around 40-50% of this waste. Experts and governmen...

item.title: Power Outages Affect Western Manitoba Due to High Winds and Dust Storms
item.description: On May 14, 2026, southwestern Manitoba experienced widespread power outages caused by strong winds and dust storm conditions. Manitoba Hydro reported that conditions became too dangerous for crews to ...
